posts.text:
If you are counting Strait of Hormuz barrels, don’t forget to add the bypass pipelines if you want an apples-to-apples comparison to pre-war levels. 

What matters is not SoH flows, but Persian Gulf flows. As long as the bypass pipelines continue to be used (and they are), SoH
posts.summary: When analyzing Persian Gulf oil exports, bypass pipelines that circumvent the Strait of Hormuz should be included in flow calculations to accurately compare current volumes against pre-war levels. The total Persian Gulf output, rather than Strait of Hormuz transit alone, is the relevant metric for understanding actual oil market supply.

posts.text:
Kpler's drone-based tank level data is sending a clear warning signal at cushing

The US crude market's most-watched inventory hub has shed around 11 million barrels since early May, and at the current draw pace, stocks are on course to test operational tank bottoms by mid-July. https://t.co/OHrryNIn62
posts.summary: Cushing crude inventories have declined by approximately 11 million barrels since early May and are projected to reach operational minimum levels by mid-July, potentially signaling tightening supply conditions in the US crude market's key storage hub.

posts.text:
Strait of Hormuz crossings rebound

Kpler’s rolling coverage of US-Iran diplomatic developments shows a clear shift in Strait of Hormuz activity across two consecutive weekends. Between 12–14 June, total crossings reached 32. One week later, from 19–21 June, crossings rose to 93 https://t.co/L03f6wFOx1
posts.summary: Strait of Hormuz crossings nearly tripled from 32 to 93 between mid-June and late June, suggesting reduced tensions in US-Iran relations that could stabilize global oil supply flows through this critical chokepoint.

posts.text:
Iran Rushes to Woo Asia’s Largest Oil Importers After US Waiver

Sellers including middlemen and representatives from the National Iranian Oil Co. made contact with refiners in India, Japan, South Korea and elsewhere even before the license was officially granted, according to
posts.summary: Iran is actively reaching out to major Asian oil importers following a US waiver, with Iranian oil sellers and officials contacting refiners in India, Japan, and South Korea to resume or expand oil sales. This suggests Iran is moving quickly to capitalize on the licensing opportunity to increase crude exports to Asia's largest markets.

posts.text: The Hormuz crisis is triggering a global race to build oil reserves. From India and Australia to Singapore and Pakistan, governments are expanding storage capacity to boost energy security, potentially creating demand for up to 1 billion barrels over the coming years. #Oil
posts.summary: Concerns about supply disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz are prompting multiple countries to build strategic petroleum reserves, which could generate significant demand for approximately 1 billion barrels of oil in coming years. This reserve-building initiative may support oil prices and benefit producers while enhancing energy security for participating nations.

It focuses on gold prices declining toward $4,100 due to expectations of higher funding costs from the Fed's inflation focus, a firm dollar, and rising bond yields, but makes no specific reference to oil market implications.

posts.text:
🎙️ NEW PODCAST 🎧

On the latest episode of Oil Ground Up, I was joined by @mercoglianos to talk about the nitty gritty of what drove shipping behaviour through the Hormuz crisis and how long it will take to normalize flows.

posts.summary: Rory Johnston discusses shipping behavior during the Hormuz crisis and the timeline for normalizing oil flows on his Oil Ground Up podcast with a guest contributor.

posts.text: The Strait of Hormuz may be reopening, but tanker markets remain in crisis mode. Daily tanker rates have nearly doubled to over $190,000, while some VLCCs are reportedly earning up to $470,000 per day as vessel shortages persist. #Oil #Tankers #Hormuz #Shipping
posts.summary: Despite potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates have surged dramatically due to ongoing vessel shortages, with daily rates exceeding $190,000 and some VLCCs reaching $470,000 per day. This supply-demand imbalance in shipping capacity is creating significant cost pressures on oil transportation markets.

The average of the twelve monthly futures contracts for delivery in 2027 (commonly called the https://t.co/8M93enUQVU

posts.text:
Global oil trade, including oil, condensate and refined products, is expected to rise by nearly 25% by 2050. 

Interregional crude and condensate trade stood at around 37.3 mb/d in 2025. By 2030, it is expected to increase to 41.8 mb/d, supported by rising oil demand in major https://t.co/qjExFbxenp
posts.summary: OPEC projects global oil trade to grow nearly 25% by 2050, with interregional crude and condensate trade increasing from 37.3 million barrels per day in 2025 to 41.8 million barrels per day by 2030, driven by rising oil demand.

posts.text:
Approvals for new gas-fired power plants rose to a 25-year high last year

Until 2023, most gas-fired power plant approvals were in gas-importing countries, but the US now dominates orders, driven by data centre demand

Learn more in our report ➡️ https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/DBX0xeI2as
posts.summary: Gas-fired power plant approvals hit a 25-year high in 2023, with the US leading new orders driven by data center demand, signaling increased reliance on natural gas for electricity generation. This trend suggests sustained demand for natural gas as a fuel source, which could have implications for energy markets and the competitive dynamics between natural gas and other fuel types.

posts.text: UAE’s exit from OPEC+ reduced the group’s share of crude oil production and capacity. #TodayInEnergy https://t.co/n7FqyRemAh https://t.co/4c2K6dGU95
posts.summary: The UAE's departure from OPEC+ has decreased the organization's overall share of global crude oil production and capacity. This reduction weakens OPEC+'s market influence and production control.

posts.text:
🚨 ETHANOL DEMAND 2026: The next leg of growth may not come from Washington.

Generational blending economics and global energy security concerns create fresh demand opportunities for US ethanol.

posts.summary: US ethanol demand may experience growth by 2026 driven by improved blending economics and global energy security needs, rather than relying on government policy support. This shift could create new market opportunities for American ethanol producers beyond traditional Washington-dependent demand drivers.

posts.text: Keep an eye on European refiners this week. They are NOT configured to operate when ambient temperatures are above 100 degrees Fahrenheit.  Lower yields are likely across the Continent.
posts.summary: European refineries are expected to experience reduced output this week due to high ambient temperatures exceeding their operational limits, which could tighten crude oil processing capacity across the continent. Lower refinery yields may support higher energy prices amid constrained supply.

posts.text:
European gas markets don't move in isolation. Storage dynamics, shifting LNG flows, pipeline policy, geopolitical risk. It all connects, and it all moves fast.

EA Live Europe Gas brings you real-time updates and expert analysis from our global team, tracking how worldwide https://t.co/6LVy7dp4OY
posts.summary: Energy Aspects highlights how European gas market dynamics—including storage levels, LNG flows, and pipeline policy—are interconnected and volatile, factors that can have spillover effects on broader energy markets including oil pricing through commodity correlation and geopolitical risk transmission.

posts.text: Interesting analysis from Goldman on EVs. They estimate total hit to global oil demand of 130,000-320,000 b/d by end-2027 despite accelerated sales that have pushed EV share of vehicle market up 3.4% last month to 26.1% (all-time high).
posts.summary: Goldman Sachs estimates that accelerating EV adoption will reduce global oil demand by 130,000-320,000 barrels per day by the end of 2027, despite electric vehicles reaching an all-time market share of 26.1%. The relatively modest projected impact on oil demand suggests that EV growth, while significant, will only modestly offset overall petroleum consumption through 2027.

posts.text:
We have more insight than ever. So why are decisions getting harder? 

Markets are more volatile. Decisions more complex. The cost of delay is higher than ever. 

The challenge isn’t knowing more. It’s acting, quickly and with clarity. 

CERA Titan: https://t.co/vhIqgYP6tU https://t.co/lt4iZvjVMw
posts.summary: S&P Global Energy highlights that despite having unprecedented market data, energy sector decision-makers face greater complexity and volatility, making swift and decisive action more critical than ever. The core challenge for the industry has shifted from gathering information to executing decisions quickly in an increasingly uncertain market environment.

posts.text: Rubio: Iran will not be allowed to charge tolls in Strait of Hormuz under any final deal https://t.co/HuG0Qkr17U

posts.text:
Stay ahead of #HurricaneSeason with our revamped Interactive U.S. Gulf Coast Refinery & Hurricane Path Map! Track storms, receive instant alerts, and safeguard your supply chain from #GulfCoast disruptions. Register for free access: https://t.co/ykpjmZmczV

The tool offers real-time storm tracking and alerts to help market participants mitigate potential disruptions to Gulf Coast refining operations.
